title: ferrotorch-core: special::erf / erfc / digamma / gelu_none f64 polynomial approximation residual — surfaced by phase 2.5 (#767)
description: Phase 2.5 conformance probe revealed CPU f64 erf/erfc/digamma/gelu(none) deviate from PyTorch reference at ~6e-8 (erf/erfc) and ~9e-10 (digamma) and ~5e-8 (gelu_none-via-erf) absolute error, exceeding F64_TRANSCENDENTAL = 1e-10. Root cause: ferrotorch-core/src/special.rs uses Abramowitz-Stegun 7.1.26 (documented |epsilon| <= 1.5e-7) for erf, and erf-based gelu_none inherits this. PyTorch uses libm's erf which is correctly-rounded to ~1 ulp. To close: replace the polynomial with a higher-order approximation (e.g. Cody-Hwang, Chebyshev expansion, or libm-equivalent rational) or wire libm's erf through num_traits when T = f64.
